heading: Tank vessel construction standards

Tank Vessels Over 5,000 Gross Tons To Comply Until , With Environmentally Protective Structural and Operational RequirementsJanuary 1, 2015, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–380, title IV, § 4115(b)104 Stat. 520Aug. 18, 1990“The Secretary shall, within 12 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [], complete a rulemaking proceeding and issue a final rule to require that tank vessels over 5,000 gross tons affected by , United States Code, as added by this section, comply until , with structural and operational requirements that the Secretary determines will provide as substantial protection to the environment as is economically and technologically feasible.”Aug. 18, 1990January 1, 2015section 3703a of title 46

Study on Other Structural and Operational Tank Vessel Requirements, , , as amended by , , ; , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–380, title IV, § 4115(e)104 Stat. 520Pub. L. 105–383, title IV, § 423112 Stat. 3440Pub. L. 108–293, title VII, § 705118 Stat. 1075Pub. L. 109–241, title IX, § 901(n)120 Stat. 565Aug. 18, 1990Nov. 13, 1998Aug. 9, 2004July 11, 2006“(1).—Other requirementsNot later than 6 months after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary shall determine, based on recommendations from the National Academy of Sciences or other qualified organizations, whether other structural and operational tank vessel requirements will provide protection to the marine environment equal to or greater than that provided by double hulls, and shall report to the Congress that determination and recommendations for legislative action.Aug. 18, 1990“(2).—Review and assessmentThe Secretary shall—“(A)periodically review recommendations from the National Academy of Sciences and other qualified organizations on methods for further increasing the environmental and operational safety of tank vessels;“(B)not later than 5 years after the date of enactment of this Act [], assess the impact of this section on the safety of the marine environment and the economic viability and operational makeup of the maritime oil transportation industry; andAug. 18, 1990“(C)report the results of the review and assessment to the Congress with recommendations for legislative or other action.“(3)No later than one year after the date of enactment of the Coast Guard and Maritime Transportation Act of 2004 [], the Secretary shall, taking into account the recommendations contained in the report by the Marine Board of the National Research Council entitled ‘Environmental Performance of Tanker Design in Collision and Grounding’ and dated 2001, establish and publish an environmental equivalency evaluation index (including the methodology to develop that index) to assess overall outflow performance due to collisions and groundings for double hull tank vessels and alternative designs.”Aug. 9, 2004

children.text: if it is constructed or adapted to carry, or carries, oil in bulk as cargo or cargo residue; and
when operating on the waters subject to the jurisdiction of the United States, including the Exclusive Economic Zone.
a vessel used only to respond to a discharge of oil or a hazardous substance;

a vessel of less than 5,000 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under equipped with a double containment system determined by the Secretary to be as effective as a double hull for the prevention of a discharge of oil;section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title
a vessel documented under chapter 121 of this title that was equipped with a double hull before ;August 12, 1992

a barge of less than 1,500 gross tons (as measured under chapter 145 of this title) carrying refined petroleum product in bulk as cargo in or adjacent to waters of the Bering Sea, Chukchi Sea, and Arctic Ocean and waters tributary thereto and in the waters of the Aleutian Islands and the Alaskan Peninsula west of 155 degrees west longitude; or
a vessel in the National Defense Reserve Fleet pursuant to section 57100.

children.label: B
children.text: is delivered after completion of a major conversion; or

children.label: C
children.text: had its appraised salvage value determined by the Coast Guard and is qualified for documentation as a wrecked vessel under .section 12112 of this title

A vessel of less than 5,000 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under for which a building contract or contract for major conversion was placed before , and that was delivered under that contract before , and a vessel of less than 5,000 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under that had its appraised salvage value determined by the Coast Guard before , and that qualified for documentation as a wrecked vessel under before , may not operate in the navigable waters or the Exclusive Economic Zone of the United States unless the vessel is equipped with a double hull or with a double containment system determined by the Secretary to be as effective as a double hull for the prevention of a discharge of oil.section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this titlesection 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this titlesection 12112 of this titleJune 30, 1990January 1, 1994June 30, 1990January 1, 1994

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a vessel of at least 5,000 gross tons but less than 15,000 gross tons as measured under section 14502, or an alternate tonnage measured under section 14302 as prescribed by the Secretary under section 14104, if the vessel is 25 years old or older and has a single hull, or is 30 years old or older and has a double bottom or double sides;

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a vessel of at least 15,000 gross tons but less than 30,000 gross tons as measured under section 14502, or an alternate tonnage measured under section 14302 as prescribed by the Secretary under section 14104, if the vessel is 25 years old or older and has a single hull, or is 30 years old or older and has a double bottom or double sides; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a vessel of at least 30,000 gross tons as measured under section 14502, or an alternate tonnage measured under section 14302 as prescribed by the Secretary under section 14104, if the vessel is 23 years old or older and has a single hull, or is 28 years old or older and has a double bottom or double sides.

children.label: A
children.text: a vessel that has a single hull may not operate after ; andJanuary 1, 2010

children.label: B
children.text: a vessel that has a double bottom or double sides may not operate after .January 1, 2015

children.label: d
children.text: The operation of barges described in subsection (b)(5) outside waters described in that subsection shall be on any conditions as the Secretary may require.

For the purposes of this section, the gross tonnage of a vessel shall be the gross tonnage that would have been recognized by the Secretary on , as the tonnage measured under , or as an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under .July 1, 1997section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title

This subsection does not apply to a tank vessel that, before , had undergone, or was the subject of a contract for, alterations that reduce the gross tonnage of the tank vessel, as shown by reliable evidence acceptable to the Secretary.July 1, 1997
